message:
feat(rchd): priority-based selection with cache/speed tiebreakers, cache tracker improvements

Refines the worker selection algorithm and cache tracking:

Worker selection (selection.rs, +173):

- Priority strategy now uses a three-tier scoring system:
  PRIORITY_BUCKET_SCORE (10k) for priority level,
  PRIORITY_CACHE_TIEBREAK_SCORE (1k) for warm cache bonus,
  PRIORITY_SPEED_TIEBREAK_SCORE (10) for speed hints. This gives
  deterministic tiebreaking within the same priority tier instead of
  arbitrary selection among equally-prioritized workers.

- CacheTracker::record_success now creates cache state entries for
  workers that had no prior build record (a successful run always
  implies a usable build cache). Also enforces max_projects_per_worker
  by evicting the least-recently-active project when the limit is
  exceeded.

Other refinements:

- rch-common: Cargo path dependency handling, proptest coverage, and
  type refinements.
- rch: Hook handling and TUI widget/app improvements.
- rchd/api.rs: API handler adjustments.
- README.md: Documentation updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -296,7 +296,7 @@ verify_artifacts = false
 max_transfer_mb = 2048
 
 [selection]
-strategy = "fair_fastest"
+strategy = "balanced"
 
 [self_healing]
 hook_starts_daemon = true
@@ -307,6 +307,10 @@ enabled = true
 suppress_duplicates_secs = 300
 ```
 
+Built-in worker selection defaults to `balanced`, which blends speed, load,
+health, and cache affinity. Use `priority` only when you want explicit
+worker-priority control, and `fair_fastest` when you want extra load spreading.
+
 ### Worker Config Example
 
 ```toml
